seoTitle: How to Add External Tools in Power BI (Power User Setup)
metaDescription: Learn how to add external tools to Power BI so you can plug in Python scripts, extra visualization apps, and other analyst tools alongside your reports.
intro: Power BI's External Tools feature lets you connect third-party analysis and visualization applications directly to your reports, so you're not limited to what's built into the ribbon. This guide walks through how to add and configure those tools so they show up ready to use. It's aimed at anyone who wants to move from basic reporting toward a more power-user workflow.

steps.heading: Understand what External Tools does
steps.body: External Tools gives Power BI a way to launch and connect outside applications against your current report, which is how many advanced users add things like custom analytics or visualization utilities without leaving their workflow.

steps.heading: Install and register the tool
steps.body: Each external tool needs to be installed on your machine and registered with Power BI so it appears in the External Tools ribbon the next time you open a report.

steps.heading: Configure the connection settings
steps.body: Once a tool is added, check its settings so it connects to the correct data model and behaves consistently every time you launch it from Power BI.

steps.heading: Use the tool inside your workflow
steps.body: With setup complete, you can launch the tool directly from Power BI to extend what you can do with your report, whether that's deeper analysis or a specialized visual.

steps.heading: Apply it to a real reporting scenario
steps.body: Try the setup on an actual report you maintain, such as one that could benefit from Python-based analysis or an outside visualization tool, to see the practical value.

faq.question: What are External Tools in Power BI?
faq.answer: They're third-party applications that can be registered with Power BI so you can launch them directly against your current report or data model.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything before adding an external tool?
faq.answer: Yes, most external tools need to be installed on your computer first before Power BI can detect and register them.

faq.question: Is this feature only for advanced users?
faq.answer: No, while it's often described as a power-user setup, beginners can follow the same steps to start using external tools in their reports.

faq.question: Can I use External Tools to connect Python-based analysis?
faq.answer: Yes, External Tools is commonly used to bring Python scripts and other analytical tools into a Power BI workflow.

faq.question: Will adding external tools slow down Power BI?
faq.answer: Not on its own, since the tools run separately and are only launched when you choose to open them from the ribbon.
